title: Why Prophecy Was Misunderstood #got #shorts
description: Prophecies in Westeros didn’t fail—people misunderstood them. Translation errors, bias, and belief shaped how the future was interpreted. This is why prophecy was never as clear as it seemed. #Prophecy #GameOfThrones #Shorts

scenes.narration: Prophecies in Westeros did not fail—the people who believed in them misunderstood what they were seeing.
scenes.image_prompt: ancient prophecy scroll breaking into fragments of symbols and fire, dark fantasy, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: Many prophecies were written in ancient languages, translated over time, and altered with every retelling.
scenes.image_prompt: scrolls rewriting themselves with shifting symbols and translations, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: Even a single word could change everything—like whether the prophecy spoke of a prince or a princess.
scenes.image_prompt: text morphing between prince and princess in glowing script, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: People interpreted prophecy through their own desires, seeing what they wanted to see rather than what was real.
scenes.image_prompt: figures looking into fire visions and seeing different outcomes, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: Rulers believed they were chosen, shaping their decisions around prophecy and often causing the very future they feared.
scenes.image_prompt: king staring into fire with obsession, shadows of future forming behind, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: This turned prophecy into something dangerous—a force that influenced actions instead of simply predicting them.
scenes.image_prompt: events unfolding from prophecy symbols like domino effect, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: In many cases, prophecy became self-fulfilling, shaping the world because people believed it had to come true.
scenes.image_prompt: cycle loop of events repeating from prophecy symbols, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: The future was never fixed—it was influenced by choices made in the belief that it was.
scenes.image_prompt: multiple branching timelines forming from a single point, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: This is why prophecy in Westeros feels true and false at the same time—because it depends on how it is understood.
scenes.image_prompt: half-lit world split between fire and shadow, ambiguity theme, vertical 1080x1920

scenes.narration: In the end, prophecy was never about predicting the future—it was about how people chose to act when they believed it.
scenes.image_prompt: final glowing prophecy symbol fading into darkness, philosophical ending, vertical 1080x1920
